rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE DESPINA N. KOKALIS REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST DATED MAY 13, 1993, AS AMENDED
rulings.Calendar Matter: Demurrer To Petition For Trust Contest To Set Aside And Terminate Trust, Improper Failure To Carry Out The Nicholas Kokalis Will/Inheritance Protection Trust, Fraud And Concealment Of Will Disposing Assets In 1986, And No Assets Beling To The Despina Kokalis' 1993 Living Trust, And Damages In Converting Property Belonging To Nicholas Kokalis' Children
rulings.Rulings: [Part 1 of 3] This tentative ruling is issued by Judge Michael B. McNaughton. Before the court is Angeliki Nicholas Kokalis-Zarikos' Demurrer to Maria Sotiras' Petition for Trust Contest to Set Aside and Terminate Trust, Improper Failure to Carry Out the Nicholas Kokalis Will/Inheritance Protection Trust, Fraud and Concealment of Will Disposing Assets in 1986, and No Assets Belong to the Despina Kokalis' 1993 Living Trust, and Damages in Converting Property Belonging to Nicholas Kokalis' Children ("Demurrer"). Movant's request for the court to take judicial notice of certain records - a certified certificate of death for Despina N. Kokalis, four joint tenancy deeds, and three declarations re death of joint tenant recorded on July 25, 1986 - is GRANTED, pursuant to Evidence Code sections 452 and 453. Per the Notice of Demurrer, Movant demurs generally to the Petition on grounds that it fails to state facts sufficient to state a cause of action, citing Code of Civil Procedure section 430.10(e)), and that it fails to state facts sufficient to plead a fraud cause of action against Movant. The Demurrer is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND, as follows. A demurrer is a pleading used to challenge the legal sufficiency of an opponent's pleading, based on defects that appear either on the face of the pleading under attack or from matters outside the pleading that are judicially noticeable. (See Blank v. Kirwan (1985) 39 Cal.3d 311, 318; County of Fresno v. Shelton (1998) 66 Cal.App.4th 996, 1008, 1009.) The court notes, judicial notice is limited to the relevant facts properly subject to notice given the materials presented and does not, for example, extend to the truth of assertions in those materials. (See Richtek USA, Inc. v. UPI Semiconductor Corp. (2015) 242 Cal.App.4th 651, 659-660.) Demurrers challenge only the legal sufficiency of pleadings on their face, not the truth or adequacy of factual allegations or evidence (Code of Civil Proc. sec. 430.30(a)). A ruling on a demurrer does not determine contested factual issues relating to the merits. A general demurrer is appropriate where there is a defect or defense that will defeat the claimed right to recover in whole or in part, or which discloses a complete defense, apparent on the face of the pleading, or from any matter of which the court must or may take judicial notice. (See, e.g., Cnty. of San Bernardino v. Superior Ct. (2022) 77 Cal. App. 5th 1100, 1107; Casterson v. Superior Ct. (2002) 101 Cal. App. 4th 177, 183.) "Where written documents are the foundation of an action and are attached to the complaint and incorporated therein by reference, they become a part of the complaint and may be considered on demurrer." (Qualcomm, Inc. v. Certain Underwriters at Lloyd's, London (2008) 161 Cal. App. 4th 184, 191, quoting City of Pomona v. Superior Court (2001) 89 Cal.App.4th 793, 800.) Moreover, facts appearing in exhibits attached to the complaint are given precedence over inconsistent allegations in the complaint. (Holland v. Morse Diesel Int'l, Incl. (2001) 86 Cal.App.4th 1443, 1447.)

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE DESPINA N. KOKALIS REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST DATED MAY 13, 1993, AS AMENDED
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR For The Purpose Of Posting The Tentative Ruling
rulings.Rulings: [Part 2 of 3] Here, the verified Petition alleges that decedent Despina Kokalis, as named executor in the Will of decedent Nicholas Kokalis and as trustee of the testamentary trust referred to in the Will, breached her fiduciary duties to Petitioner by, among other things, failing to distribute Nicholas Kokalis's estate in conformity with his Will which, according to Petitioner, should have been divided equally between Nicholas' three children, including Petitioner. The Petition further alleges that Despina Kokalis, under the influence of her daughter Angeliki Kokalis, breached her duties to Petitioner by creating her own Despina Kokalis Revocable Living Trust which, by its terms, distributes property unevenly between Nicholas' three children. According to Petitioner, this contradicts Nicholas' intent as expressed in Nicholas 1969 Will. The Petition fails to state facts sufficient to establish a cause of action because, on its face, Petitioner has failed to establish that Despina Kokalis owed her any such duties. Petitioner was named as a contingent beneficiary under the 1969 Will, whose right to inherit would vest "only in [the] event" that Despina predeceased Nicholas or died within sixty (60) days of Nicholas. Absent that contingency (which was not met), the 1969 Will provides that Nicholas' entire estate pass to Despina. Attached to the Petition are three exhibits, which are referred by and incorporated into the Petition's pleadings: (A) a copy of Nicholas Kokalis' Will, executed September 10, 1969; (B) a document with heading "Schedule A," listing certain real properties and stocks; and (C) a copy of Nicholas Kokalis' certificate of death, listing his date of death as March 23, 1986 and Despina Kokalis as surviving spouse. In the "Fourth" Paragraph of his 1969 Will, Nicholas stated that "I hereby give, devise and bequeath all of my estate [] to my beloved wife DESPINA KOKALIS." In the "Fifth" Paragraph, Nicholas provided for a testamentary trust "in [the] event, and only in [the] event" that Despina "should predecease me, or in the event of any accident in which [both Nicholas and Despina] should be killed [within sixty days of each other]."

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE DESPINA N. KOKALIS REVOCABLE LIVING TRUST DATED MAY 13, 1993, AS AMENDED
rulings.Calendar Matter: ADDED TO CALENDAR FOR For The Purpose Of Posting The Tentative Ruling
rulings.Rulings: [Part 3 of 3] The Petition alleges (and his Death Certificate confirms) that Nicholas died in 1986. The Petition also makes clear that Despina remained alive until at least May 13, 1993 when her own revocable living trust was established. (Petition, paragraphs 1, 3, and 6.) Thus, it appears on the face of the Petition, including its exhibits, that Despina survived Nicholas by more than 60 days, and thus, by the terms of the 1969 Will, she was entitled to inherit his entire probate estate. The judicially noticed documents further show that Despina otherwise became the sole owner of the real properties held in joint tenancy at the time of Nicholas Kokalis' death by right of survivorship. On the face of the pleadings and judicially noticed records, the contingent events necessary to activate the testamentary trust (and each of its attendant duties) set forth in paragraph Fifth of the Will never occurred. The Petition, thus fails to state facts sufficient to establish Petitioner's entitlement to any distribution or other interest under the 1969 Will or to any damages proximately caused by either Despina or Angeliki. As such, each purported cause of action fails. Insofar as the Petitioner has argued legal theories and claims in the opposition to the Demurrer that are not facially pleaded in the Petition, the Demurrer is SUSTAINED WITH LEAVE TO AMEND. In granting such leave, the Court is not expressing an opinion as to the viability of any such alternative legal theories or claims. Petitioner shall file and serve an amended petition within ten (10) days of entry of this order. Petitioner shall lodge with the clerk in Department 204 by the time set for this hearing a proposed order consistent with this tentative ruling as published above. Any party wishing to contest this tentative ruling by appearing at the scheduled hearing must so advise other parties and the courtroom clerk (415-551-3702) no later than 4:00 p.m. on the day before the hearing. To appear by Zoom, go to zoom.us and click "Join Meeting." Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765 and passcode 514879. To appear telephonically through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and follow the prompts. Enter Meeting ID number 1602254765# and passcode 514879#. Parties appearing via Zoom should use their full names in the "Your Name" section. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling CourtCall at 1-888-882-6878 and obtaining an appearance access code for the hearing's scheduled date and time in Department 204.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ALLAN M. BAIRD
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/16/2026. All of the following issues must be cleared two weeks before the hearing. Most of the issues have been posted previously: (1) The original Will has not been submitted, and the petition has not been updated to request admission of a lost will. Although the petitioner filed a declaration regarding the original will on 2/26/2026, it does not appear to support the petition. To proceed, the petitioner must submit the original Will if seeking its admission. If the petitioner intends to admit a lost will, an amended petition with the required details will likely be necessary. The Court will not resolve the issue for the petitioner (2) According to the supplement, Scott H. appointed Joseph B. and Cassandra S. as executors. The Court does not have clear authority to appoint the petitioner as executor when the named executor's written declination to act is not on file. Also, allegations of other persons that the executor contradicts the relief of this petition. Scott H's written declination to act must be filed, along with his nomination of other executors. (3) The petition does not name the trustee of the residual beneficiary trust, and proper notice to the trustee is required. The notice on file lists several beneficiaries, but addresses for Robert B., Cassandra S., and Marisa Leonis are missing. (4) The petition must clarify how the petitioner intends to address the conflict of interest resulting from their role as a creditor. (5) A full bond will be required. (6) No notices have been provided to Hodge, Joseph B., Cassandra B., or their counsel, Attorney Sun. (7) Petition #8 does not identify the heirs at law. While three relatives are listed as trust beneficiaries, the petition does not confirm they are the only heirs.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF LINDA T. HESS
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 5/4/26. If James Hess died not more than 15 years before the decedent and owned an interest in real property that passed to the decedent, and he is survived by issue of his (predeceased) parents, James Hess's three brothers are interested parties. They have not waived bond. Courtesy copies of their waivers of bond must be received by the assigned examiner by 4/24/26. If they do not waive bond, the petition could be granted with the full amount of bond ($1,460,000). Examiner continuances are final and not subject to contest under LRSF 14.10 B.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF OLAF JOHANNES LADANOWSKY
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/15/2026. Issues necessitating continuance: 1) Item 2d(3) requests that deposits in a blocked account be allowed, but Item 2d(2) requests that $20,000 bond be fixed. Deposits into blocked accounts are usually in lieu of bond. In a verified supplement, please clarify this request. 2) A minor may not nominate a personal representative, although the guardian of the estate of a person otherwise entitled to appointment may nominate a personal representative (Probate Code Section 8465(a)(3)). 3) Item 8 shows Cornelia Roppel as a proposed guardian of the person of the sole heir, Peter Jan Ladanowsky. Has a guardianship been initiated in San Francisco (or another county in California)? And if so, is there a proposed guardian of the estate? 4) A separate copy of notice must be sent to the person or persons having legal custody of the minor, with whom the minor resides (CRC 7.51(d)). With whom does the minor reside? The alleged proposed guardian was given notice at a different address than the minor. 5) All notices must include language about remote appearances (San Francisco Local Rule 14.1.C.6). Please address the above issues and delivery courtesy copies to this examiner no later 06/01/2026 to avoid additional continuance.
